title: Drain-induced bullying
intro: DIBL — drain-induced barrier lowering — is the drain reaching over and yanking the threshold voltage down whenever it feels like it. At 340 mV/V, your planar device's threshold basically belongs to the drain. Get DIBL under 50 mV/V at a sub-20 nm gate. A longer gate alone won't save you this time — you'll have to change the architecture.
explain: This single problem drove the whole transistor roadmap: planar → FinFET (Intel, 22 nm, 2011) → gate-all-around nanosheets (Samsung 3 nm, TSMC N2). Each step wraps the gate around more sides of the channel — one, three, then all four — shrinking the 'natural length' the drain field can invade. Same dimensions, different geometry: your GAA device has ~10× less DIBL than the planar one.
